description_long: This is a twin-boom, twin-engine airplane with a distinctive central fuselage and two parallel tail booms extending rearward from the wings. The aircraft features a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, and its nose and engine nacelles are painted yellow, contrasting with the blue-gray body and wings. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, with yellow bands near the tips and a slight dihedral angle. The tail section consists of twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration. The engines are mounted on the leading edge of each wing, and the propellers are positioned at the front of the nacelles. The airplane's surface appears metallic with some weathering, and it is detailed with panel lines and insignia.
description: A twin-boom, twin-engine airplane with a central fuselage, yellow nose and engine nacelles, blue-gray wings, and an H-tail configuration. The cockpit is forward-mounted and enclosed with a clear canopy.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the twin tail booms, H-tail configuration, and the horizontal stabilizer connecting the two vertical stabilizers.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the yellow nose, engine nacelles, and the straight mid-mounted wings with yellow bands.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the forward cockpit, twin engines, and the symmetrical layout of the wings and tail booms.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the yellow nose, engine nacelles, and the detailed panel lines on the fuselage and wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral angle and no winglets; twin-boom layout.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the leading edge of each wing.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main gear under each engine nacelle and a nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely single or tandem seat.
